mechanism_narrative: A persistent low level of the federal funds rate maintained over recent months signals accommodative monetary policy that supports equity valuations through low discount rates, leading to positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% is not statistically greater than zero over any 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window, it signals deteriorating labor demand that reduces household income expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the rank correlation between UNRATE change and returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production indicate a broad contraction in aggregate demand and supply activity; the joint deterioration amplifies recession risk beyond either series alone, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and INDPRO decline, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power relative to main effects (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of near-zero interest rates, an acceleration in consumer price inflation above recent trend signals potential early monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns; this effect is absent when rates are elevated.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, CPIAUCSL acceleration fails to predict negative returns (mean forward return not negative, t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism exhibits equal strength when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts reflects strengthening residential investment and construction employment, which boosts cyclical earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive momentum in HOUST fails to correlate with positive 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls by more than 1% over a two-month span, it signals a factory sector contraction that reduces corporate profit margins and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal rank correlation is positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth slows while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises, the joint deterioration in labor market breadth and joblessness signals a cyclical downturn that is deeper than either indicator suggests alone, generating negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of slowing PAYEMS and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns (mean return >= 0 over triggered periods), or the interaction term does not improve prediction over main effects (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth serves as a comprehensive measure of aggregate domestic demand; stronger than expected GDP momentum lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to positively predict 60-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of high-growth quarters.
